data.q: What is the minimum recommended contrast ratio between text and its background according to WCAG AA guidelines?
data.e: WCAG AA requires a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text to ensure readability.

data.q: Which WCAG level has the strictest contrast ratio requirement of 7:1 for normal text?
data.e: WCAG AAA requires a contrast ratio of 7:1 for normal-sized text to ensure higher accessibility.

data.q: Which tool can be used to measure color contrast on web pages?
data.e: All these tools help measure WCAG color contrast and accessibility compliance.

data.q: If a text color contrasts poorly with its background, what is the best approach to improve accessibility?
o: Increase contrast by adjusting text or background colors
data.e: Increasing contrast between text and background colors improves readability and accessibility.

o: World Consortium for Accessibility in Graphs
data.e: WCAG stands for Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, which provide standards for creating accessible web content.

data.q: In terms of color contrast, what does a ratio of 4.5:1 mean?
o: The text is 4.5 times bigger than the background
The luminance difference between text and background meets the minimum accessibility requirement
The color saturation is reduced by 4.5 times
The font weight is multiplied by 4.5
data.e: A 4.5:1 contrast ratio means the difference in relative luminance between two colors meets WCAG minimum standards.

data.q: Which text size can use a lower contrast ratio (3:1) under WCAG AA?
o: Large text of at least 18pt or 14pt bold
data.e: Large text can have a contrast ratio of 3:1 instead of 4.5:1 when meeting WCAG AA.

data.q: WCAG contrast guidelines help users with:
data.e: Proper contrast helps users with various disabilities including low vision and color blindness.

data.q: Why is sufficient color contrast important in web development?
o: To make the website load faster
To improve visibility and readability for all users
data.e: Sufficient contrast improves readability and usability across different user conditions and devices.

data.q: Which of these is NOT a recommended practice to ensure good color contrast?
o: Rely solely on color to convey information
Provide text labels along with color coding
data.e: Relying solely on color to convey information is discouraged because users with vision impairments might miss it.
